Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SSE realtime: use direct Postgres connection for LISTEN/NOTIFY

PgBouncer in transaction pooling mode doesn't support LISTEN/NOTIFY.
SSEHub now uses direct pgx.Conn connections (via DATABASE_URL_DIRECT)
instead of acquiring from the pooled connection, bypassing PgBouncer.

This also frees pool connections — LISTEN goroutines no longer consume
pool slots, so MaxConns can be reduced.

fix: align HA integration across bot & web, target both locks

Both bot and web now use zwave_js/set_lock_usercode and
zwave_js/clear_lock_usercode directly, replacing the bot's old
HA script approach. A shared HA_LOCK_ENTITIES env var (comma-separated
entity IDs) drives which locks get updated — calls are made in parallel
so both front and back door always stay in sync.

  /mycode troubleshooting, HA_LOCK_ENTITIES setup instructions,
  complete migrations list

commit.message:
feat: add gravity wells, signal spillover, momentum tracking, and route optimizer

- Gravity: high-score cells pull residue from lower-score face neighbors
  each tick, creating semantic "gravity wells" that concentrate activity
- Spillover: signal ingestion spills 8% of score delta to face neighbors,
  creating natural heat spread across the semantic field
- Momentum: tracks velocity of score changes per cell with history,
  enabling detection of rapidly heating/cooling regions
- Route optimizer: greedy DFS computes top-K optimal traversal paths
  through the cube from any starting cell, maximizing cumulative score
- New API endpoints: GET /api/clashd27/gravity, GET /api/clashd27/routes/:cellId
- Updated emergence snapshot with gravityWells, momentum, optimalRoutes
- ASCII renderer now shows gravity wells (G) and heating cells (^)
- 49 tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The full requirements.txt caused version conflicts during Docker build
(opentelemetry, wrapt, protobuf backtracking). The bootstrap server
only needs websockets, fastapi, uvicorn, and pydantic.

- Rewrite Dockerfile.bootstrap as single-stage, no cache mounts
- Use minimal prsm/__init__.py (avoids importing heavy core deps)
- Use curl-based healthcheck instead of httpx

commit.message:
Fix WHT bit-ordering bug; rerun sweep with corrected Hamiltonian

The WHT coefficient for Z-string S was being assigned to all_wires[k]
(LSB-first) when it should map to all_wires[n-1-k] (MSB-first), matching
the truth table's ordering where all_wires[0] is the most significant bit.

The eigenvalue multiset was unchanged (which is why the earlier verification
passed), but the diagonal was permuted: the optimizer was minimising the
wrong Hamiltonian. ma-QAOA appeared to achieve AR=1.0 while actually
measuring bad states. QAOA was unaffected (uses qml.Hermitian, not WHT).

Fix verified: diagonal of reconstructed Hamiltonian now matches outcomes
element-by-element (max diff = 0.0) and agrees exactly with qml.pauli_decompose.

Update vcg_results.md with corrected timing and AR numbers from rerun.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add research gravity, discovery candidates, and event emitter

Implements three new modules for the CLASHD27 discovery engine:
- research-gravity.js: computes gravitational hotspots across 27-cell cube
  based on collisions, clusters, gradients, corridors, and residue pressure
- discovery-candidates.js: detects cross-domain discovery opportunities by
  combining gravity, emergence patterns, and collision data
- event-emitter.js: packages algorithm output into structured events

commit.message:
fix: corrigir get_all_descendants para não incluir o próprio nó

Corrige bug onde a função get_all_descendants estava incluindo
o próprio nó pai na lista de descendentes, causando marcação duplicada
e desnecessária.

A lógica correta é:
- Percorrer apenas os filhos do nó
- Adicionar apenas os descendentes (filhos, netos, etc.)
- NÃO adicionar o próprio nó pai

 Isso garante que quando marcamos um tópico pai, apenas seus
 descendentes são marcados automaticamente.

commit.message:
fix: OG 이미지 추출 시 InputStream.read()를 readNBytes()로 교체

InputStream.read(byte[])는 버퍼를 끝까지 채우는 것을 보장하지 않아
HTML이 불완전하게 읽혀 og:image 파싱에 실패하는 문제 수정.
readNBytes(n)는 최대 n 바이트를 보장하여 안정적인 썸네일 추출 가능.

fix: harden QML plugin, bridge error handling, and tool guards

- Guard all handlers against null cursor.measure/cursor.segment

- Propagate navigation errors in read_passage and transpose_passage

Tests (152 total, +7 new):
- Happy-path tests for set_barline, set_key_signature, add_chord_symbol

commit.message:
feat: detect market resolution and compute actual payout P/L

When a Polymarket contract settles (YES→$1, NO→$0), the trader gets the
payout price — not our TP/SL. This adds a new 'market_resolved' resolution
type that detects when a signal's market is no longer actively trading and
the price is at an extreme ($0.95+ or $0.05-).

P/L is now computed as (payout - entry) / entry, reflecting actual trader
returns. Market-resolved signals count as wins or losses in metrics based
on whether payout > entry.

Dashboard shows "SETTLED WIN" / "SETTLED LOSS" labels with the W/L/S/E
breakdown and purple slice in the donut chart.

commit.message:
Revert/delete RPC, per-file import progress, cancel bug fix, and disputed status

- Move revertImport() and deleteImport() into PostgreSQL RPC functions
  (revert_import_data, delete_import_data) to eliminate N+1 orphan detection
  loop. 200-customer import: 1200+ Supabase round trips → 1 RPC call.
  Add idx_stitching_conflicts_import index.
- Replace combined multi-file import progress bar with individual per-file
  progress bars and percentages in the table status column.
- Fix cancel bug: add multiImportAbortRef so the async import loop stops
  when Cancel is clicked. Previously the old loop's functional state updates
  would corrupt the new file queue after re-uploading.
- Add "disputed" to payment_status enum (DB, schema validation, TS type,
  import cast) so Stripe CSVs with disputed charges import without errors.

commit.message: feat(data-browsing): Use custom React chart legend component in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization` (#108493) Replaces the default ECharts SVG legend with an interactive React component in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization`. The ECharts legend has usability issues — users don't realize items are clickable, and the scrollable ribbon doesn't handle overflow well. This custom version is more powerful. It sits in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization` because it needs to rely on and be aware of various time series coordination problems (colour palettes, etc.) that don't apply in other contexts. Maybe in the future I can expand this to be used in other places, but the main motivator by far is to use this in Dashboards and Explore. **e.g.,** The new `ChartLegend` renders a row of colored checkboxes that toggle series visibility. Items that don't fit are kept in the DOM (hidden with `visibility: hidden` so they still contribute to width measurement) and shown in an overflow dropdown instead. A `ResizeObserver` on both the container and the overflow trigger re-measures whenever available space changes. State updates from the observer are deferred via `scheduleMicroTask` to avoid forced reflows. [Improved Chart Legend](https://linear.app/getsentry/project/improved-chart-legend-e0d2083d7197/overview) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude

commit.message:
fix(fetchers): add VPS Playwright scraper for Bybit/Bitget WAF bypass

Bybit: All direct API endpoints (api2.bybit.com, www.bybit.com) are
blocked by Akamai WAF from datacenter IPs globally. New approach uses
VPS SG Playwright scraper that visits bybitglobal.com (not WAF-protected)
and fetches the beehive API via page.evaluate(fetch) with session cookies.
Supports multi-page bulk fetch for efficiency.

Bitget: Public V1 API decommissioned, V2 endpoints return 404. Added
VPS Playwright scraper as additional fallback that intercepts API responses
from the browser-rendered leaderboard page.

Strategy order: VPS Playwright scraper > direct API > CF Worker > VPS proxy > www fallback
